retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Spacers can be used before or during braces treatment to increase space interproximally. Spacers are normally utilized between molars if there is not enough space for metal bands to be placed around them. Spacers can be made of different materials based on how long they need to be placed in the mouth. Rubber spacers are traditionally used for 1 to 2 weeks and normally fall out as soon as space is adequate. Metal spacers are commonly used for more than six weeks but can also be used for short-term space management. [23]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: While various methods have been proven to treat malocclusion successfully, newer technologies are still being tested to improve the effectiveness and efficiency of treatment. Perhaps in the near future, the utilization of low-level laser therapy on orthodontic movement, for example, will be a common practice in orthodontic offices. [27]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: There are several methods to treat malocclusion. Palatal expanders are traditionally used before braces treatment to treat a narrow palate, crowding, or crossbite. Rapid palatal expansion (RPE) works by placing a force on the two maxillary bones to split the mid-palatal suture. Palatal expanders are most useful during adolescence, as the maxillary bones become fused during puberty. The expander contains a screw turned daily or as directed by the patient's orthodontist. Every turn places pressure on the maxillary bone, allowing for less than 1 mm of expansion. [22]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of malocclusion. Describe the etiology behind malocclusion. Identify the different classes of malocclusion. Apply best practices when treating malocclusion. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Each case of malocclusion treatment is variable, as it depends on the severity of the case, patient compliance, amount of space available in both jaws, and the overall oral health of the patient. [29]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Over the past few years, clear aligners have become increasingly popular, especially in adult patients. Clear aligners utilize CAD/CAM technology to digitally scan the dental arches to create clear removable trays. A clear aligner treatment has many different sets of aligners, each for a different stage of treatment. Patients wear each stage tray for 1-2 weeks, depending on the treatment plan created by the orthodontist.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Anterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: The type of movement required for correction is assessed. Removable appliances can be considered for tipping movement; the fixed appliance is indicated for bodily tooth movement. The appliance should incorporate these features and good anterior retention to counteract the displaying effect of the active element. A bite plane or an active component could move the teeth to free the occlusion with the opposing arch. Fixed appliances can be indicated when there is insufficient overbite to retain the corrected incisors. Open coil springs can be used in straight wire mechanics to create enough arch length to position the teeth. A negative root torque is sometimes required for palatally placed upper incisors. An adequate overbite and a normal inclination of the long axis of the tooth to be treated are important for retention stability.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: The coffin spring is an omega-shaped wire appliance that corrects crossbite in young, developing dentition. The expansion produced is slow and bilaterally symmetrical.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: Fixed orthodontic appliances can be used to correct posterior crossbites, as they provide 3-dimensional control over the tooth. The arches can be kept slightly expanded or constricted, depending upon the movement required. Cross-elastics of 3/16-inch diameter exerting a force of 2.5 to 4.5 oz can correct individual tooth crossbites in the posterior segment.

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Enhancing Healthcare Team Outcomes
retrieved_docs.content: Dental hygienists and general dentists play a critical role in monitoring the oral health and state of disease, if applicable, of the patient receiving orthodontic treatment. Many patients who receive traditional braces have a tougher time practicing proper oral hygiene. One of the most commonly neglected sites is the interproximal posterior areas of dentition. Gingivitis may develop if plaque builds up over time in the patient's supragingival and subgingival pockets. Depending on the severity of the disease, oral hygiene instruction and prophylaxis or scaling and root planing may need to be performed by the dentist or dental hygienist. [33] [Level 5]

retrieved_docs.title: Oral Surgery, Extraction of Teeth -- Indications -- Orthodontic Extractions
retrieved_docs.content: Teeth are often considered for extraction for orthodontic reasons, often to create space in the dental arch to allow movement of the teeth into a more favorable position. [21] A plan for which teeth are to be removed should be formulated by an orthodontist.

retrieved_docs.title: Pierre Robin Syndrome -- Treatment / Management
retrieved_docs.content: Mandibular distraction osteogenesis is another surgical intervention that produces longer-term results. This procedure advances and elongates the jaw in 3 phases - latency, activation, and consolidation. Latency is the period of time after osteotomy, and before distraction begins with the rotation of an external device that elongates the distance between the bones at the site of the break. Consolidation is the time allowed for bone formation and healing at the site of the osteotomy. Complications include infection, osteomyelitis of the mandible, damage to the inferior alveolar nerve, bite deformities, and permanent dentition loss. [2] [11]

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Review Questions
retrieved_docs.content: Access free multiple choice questions on this topic. Click here for a simplified version. Comment on this article.

retrieved_docs.title: Orthodontics, Malocclusion -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Malocclusion often develops when a child begins transitioning into mixed dentition. Malocclusion can cause speech problems, difficulty eating, changes in facial structure, and tongue and cheek biting. Since there is no known etiology behind malocclusion, an interprofessional team approach is crucial to allow for an earlier diagnosis. Diagnosis is standardized with the use of classification systems and radiographs. Orthodontists use several methods to treat malocclusion, including palatal expanders, spacers, braces, and clear aligners. This activity defines and distinguishes the different types of malocclusion seen in orthodontics and highlights the role of the interprofessional team in diagnosing and utilizing various techniques to treat malocclusion.

retrieved_docs.title: Posterior Crossbite -- Treatment / Management -- Management of Posterior Crossbite
retrieved_docs.content: A Quad Helix is a fixed appliance soldered to molar bands cemented to the first permanent molars. It can produce slow expansion and be used with a fixed appliance. The forces generated by the appliance can be controlled depending on the amount of activation. Reactivation is done using 3-prong pliers.

retrieved_docs.title: Stainless Steel Crowns in Primary Dentition -- Technique or Treatment -- Adjacent Stainless Steel Crown With Arch Length Loss/Space Loss
retrieved_docs.content: Reduction of the crown's mesiodistal dimension is frequently required, particularly when mesial drift (often caused by caries) has resulted in arch length loss. Flattening the crown's mesial and distal contact areas using Adam pliers results in a moderate reduction in the mesiodistal dimension. A contralateral upper tooth crown with a smaller size can be used if there is mesial drift in the lower arch. Another form of modification is vertical slicing and spot-welding, an additional segment of a stainless steel band. This is done to increase the perimeter or extend the length. However, the efficacy of the latter modifications remains largely untested. [8]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1930
retrieved_docs.content: The term “Other OR therapeutic procedures on musculoskeletal system” functions as a classification label for the broader category “other or therapeutic procedures on musculoskeletal system.” Within this classified group, the specific intervention “Reopening of osteotomy site of facial bone” is regarded as a related and possibly synonymous procedure, indicating that it belongs to the same set of musculoskeletal therapeutic actions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2783
retrieved_docs.content: Physical therapy exercises; manipulation; and other procedures are identified as a distinct category that classifies itself, indicating that these therapeutic interventions are grouped together under a single classification for clinical and therapeutic purposes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 2 -- Community 1184
retrieved_docs.content: effects as specific conditions associated with the concept of “كسر,” situates them within a broader category of unspecified injury sequelae, and further delineates limb‑specific fracture sequelae, thereby providing a structured framework for understanding and managing the long‑term consequences of various bone injuries.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 8 -- Level 2 -- Community 1174
retrieved_docs.content: shared therapeutic goals for musculoskeletal and connective‑tissue conditions. This integrated framework supports comprehensive patient care by aligning specific modalities with appropriate clinical indications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 11 -- Level 3 -- Community 2204
retrieved_docs.content: Other OR therapeutic procedures on musculoskeletal system is a classification term that categorizes other or therapeutic procedures on the musculoskeletal system, grouping together various interventions aimed at treating musculoskeletal conditions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 5 -- Community 2946
retrieved_docs.content: The category “الإجراءات العلاجية، غير مصنف في موضع آخر” (therapeutic procedures, not classified elsewhere) is positioned as a subcategory within the broader domain of “المعالجة التأهيلية” (rehabilitative therapy), indicating that these uncategorized therapeutic procedures are considered part of rehabilitative treatment approaches.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2770
retrieved_docs.content: The allowed qualifier “rehabilitace” is applicable as a therapeutic approach for the conditions قرحة قلاعية, الالتصاق, and اِضطِراب اللسان, indicating that rehabilitation can be employed in the management of these oral health disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 1 -- Community 256
retrieved_docs.content: diverse injury types (intracranial, skeletal, facial), their specific anatomical subcategories, corresponding treatment modalities, and long‑term sequelae are interrelated, while also incorporating psychosocial determinants such as aggression that may influence injury occurrence and outcome. This integrated framework supports comprehensive assessment, classification, and management of patients presenting with complex traumatic or toxic presentations.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 2 -- Level 1 -- Community 250
retrieved_docs.content: The extraction of a single tooth is a direct dental procedure performed on the zuby (teeth), involving the removal of one tooth from the oral cavity.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 2 -- Community 1193
retrieved_docs.content: interventions and general rehabilitative exercise therapies are both relevant for managing musculoskeletal conditions. This integrated hierarchy underscores how thermal and ultrasound modalities, exercise‑based interventions, and broader rehabilitative categories interconnect to support comprehensive treatment strategies across ophthalmic, connective‑tissue, and musculoskeletal disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 1 -- Community 259
retrieved_docs.content: and to the neurosurgical and spinal explorations. Additional Excision applications include therapeutic procedures involving the nose, mouth and pharynx and are related to plastic procedures on the nose. Together, Exploration provides an umbrella framework for targeted surgical investigations, while Excision represents the core operative technique that enables tissue removal, obstruction relief, diagnostic assessment and neural decompression, thereby optimizing patient outcomes across multiple specialties.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2107
retrieved_docs.content: Skull and face fractures, described in Arabic as كسر في قبو القحف, refer to injuries involving breaks in the cranial vault and the facial bones, encompassing a range of traumatic damage to the head and facial structures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1924
retrieved_docs.content: not considered forms of Excision. Excision is also linked to procedures involving the head and neck region. It is identified as the “method_of” other or therapeutic procedures on the nose, mouth, and pharynx, and it has a relationship with plastic procedures on the nose, suggesting that excisional techniques are employed both in therapeutic and aesthetic interventions of the nasal area. Overall, the knowledge base positions Excision as a central operative modality encompassing a wide range of minimally invasive and open surgical techniques across orthopedics, general surgery, neurosurgery, and endocrine surgery, while simultaneously defining its boundaries by excluding specific laminectomy approaches and connecting it to both therapeutic and plastic procedures of the facial region.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2082
retrieved_docs.content: Crushing injuries or internal injuries are associated with open wounds (جرح مفتوح), indicating that such traumatic forces often result in visible tissue disruption.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 2430
retrieved_docs.content: Other miscellaneous procedures are classified as a subset of other nonoperative procedures, indicating that they fall under the broader category of nonoperative interventions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 4 -- Community 2789
retrieved_docs.content: المعالجة بالتمارين (exercise therapy) is organized under the broader category of physical therapy exercises, manipulation, and other procedures, indicating that it encompasses these various physical therapy modalities as its parent techniques. This relationship highlights that exercise therapy integrates multiple therapeutic actions—including specific exercises, manual manipulation, and additional procedural interventions—to provide a comprehensive approach to patient rehabilitation and functional improvement.
